description_long: This object is a vintage-style cathode ray tube (CRT) television with a bulky, boxy form and a deep chassis extending far behind the screen. The front features a large, slightly curved glass display surrounded by a thick, matte black plastic bezel. Below the screen, there are several physical control buttons and a small logo, with the brand name 'TECNO TOWER' visible on the lower right. The sides and top of the television are solid and feature ventilation slits for heat dissipation. The back panel is recessed and contains a set of input/output ports, as well as additional ventilation grilles. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and function, typical of older television models found in urban environments, garages, or curbside settings.
description: A bulky, black CRT television with a curved glass screen, thick plastic bezel, front control buttons, and rear ventilation grilles and ports.
description_view_0: Front view showing the curved glass screen, thick bezel, control buttons, and visible brand logo.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the deep, boxy chassis and solid matte surface with no visible features.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the ventilation slits on top and the recessed panel with input/output ports at the bottom.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the depth and solid construction of the television.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, matte black plastic bezel with angular corners and a subtle logo on the lower edge.
attribute_control_button_layout: row of buttons on the lower front bezel, left of center
